posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
change the name to Overjoy and that’d be my assessment. The pale dilute color belies the aromatic intensity and the layers of flavor. Really enjoyable.

name corrected since I was reprimanded to do so.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: The other Overnoy’s Ploussard is similarly delicious in 2015. A bit more mid-palate weight and sweet fruit than usual, but very enjoyable.

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked: Getting to be a hipster alan. Overnoy? Cornas aged in concrete? What next?!?

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
c fu:

Getting to be a hipster alan. Overnoy? Cornas aged in concrete? What next?!?

aspiring to be you!

posts.name: Mike Evans
posts.cooked:
maureen nelson:

Now you’re getting Disorderly!

It would be much more Disorderly if it was a Pierre Overnoy (or at this point, Emmanuel Houillon). I think the subject line is misleading as the only connection between Overnoy-Crinquand and the more storied domaine is a distant family relationship. It is simply incorrect to refer to Overnoy-Crinquand as Overnoy.
^ correct. This is not the Overnoy-Houillon poulsard. That being said, 2015 Jura poulsard is inherently a very low yield vintage so likely what is out there and available is probably pretty tasty!

posts.name: Мајкл Ристев
posts.cooked:
actually drank the overnoy houillon 15 and it was already oxidized. it was direct from the importer too

15 ganevat was a bit too much bubble gum for me, preferred the 14

posts.name: Ramon Cabrera
posts.cooked: Had this this been Overnoy-Houillon, he would have been Uberjoyed.

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
AlbertH:

^ correct. This is not the Overnoy-Houillon poulsard. That being said, 2015 Jura poulsard is inherently a very low yield vintage so likely what is out there and available is probably pretty tasty!

modified the name. I don’t know enough to know I had erred. Regardless, I had provided a picture of the label and it’s still a darn nice wine.

posts.name: Nathan Smyth
posts.cooked: Well there it is at VIVC, Ploussard = Poulsard:

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked:
alan weinberg:
AlbertH:

^ correct. This is not the Overnoy-Houillon poulsard. That being said, 2015 Jura poulsard is inherently a very low yield vintage so likely what is out there and available is probably pretty tasty!

modified the name. I don’t know enough to know I had erred. Regardless, I had provided a picture of the label and it’s still a darn nice wine.

I take it back. You’re no longer on the road to being a hipster.

Apparently this is almost as bad as the time my friend sent me an email offering me Domaine Leflaive Montrachet through his distributor and when I said yes… it was The big O Leflaive

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
c fu:
alan weinberg:
AlbertH:

^ correct. This is not the Overnoy-Houillon poulsard. That being said, 2015 Jura poulsard is inherently a very low yield vintage so likely what is out there and available is probably pretty tasty!

modified the name. I don’t know enough to know I had erred. Regardless, I had provided a picture of the label and it’s still a darn nice wine.

I take it back. You’re no longer on the road to being a hipster.

Apparently this is almost as bad as the time my friend sent me an email offering me Domaine Leflaive Montrachet through his distributor and when I said yes… it was The big O Leflaive

well, I had my 15 minutes. But, hey, you saw the label . . .

If you can secure a bottle - Overnoy-Houillon is worth trying, Alan…the poulsard is to the Jura as Truchot is the Cote d’Or . It is an amazing bottle of wine.

-mark

posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked:
Alan I did the same thing with the 2010 O-C Savagnin. Haven’t cracked it yet.

Cheers,
Doug
